atproto.details: Local AT-URI parser uses permissive string splitting without proper validation. Missing: (1) 8192 char length limit, (2) strict regex validation, (3) proper authority validation via ParseAtIdentifier, (4) proper NSID validation for collection, (5) proper RecordKey validation. Accepts invalid URIs like 'at://did:plc:test//app.bsky.feed.post/abc' (double slash) and 'at://did:plc:test/app.bsky.feed.post/' (trailing slash with empty rkey).
atproto.impact: Security: May accept malformed AT-URIs from untrusted sources. Interop: May generate/proxy invalid AT-URIs to other services causing failures.
atproto.reproducibility: Run unit tests with invalid URIs - current parser will accept them

atproto.details: Local NSID parser only checks for non-empty string containing a period. Missing: (1) 317 char limit, (2) regex validation, (3) segment length validation (63 chars max per segment). Accepts invalid NSIDs like 'a' (no period), 'com..example' (empty segment), or very long segments.
atproto.impact: Interop: May accept invalid NSIDs for collection names. Will fail when interacting with strict ATProto services.
atproto.reproducibility: Test with invalid NSIDs - current parser will accept them

atproto.details: Local RecordKey parser only checks for non-empty and no '/'. Missing: (1) 512 char limit, (2) regex validation, (3) rejection of '.' and '..' as record keys. Accepts invalid rkeys like '.' and '..' which are explicitly forbidden by spec.
atproto.impact: Security: Accepts '.' and '..' record keys which are reserved. Interop: May generate invalid record references.
atproto.reproducibility: Test with '.' or '..' as rkey - current parser will accept them

atproto.details: Firehose event parser does NOT handle #sync events. The switch statement handles #commit, #identity, #account, #info but NOT #sync. According to subscribeRepos.json, #sync is part of the message union and is required for 'inductive' firehose consumption.
atproto.impact: Functionality: Cannot process sync events which indicate repo state recovery. May lose track of repos after data recovery events.
atproto.reproducibility: Inspect source at firehose.go:89-121 - no case for 'sync'

atproto.details: Local DID parser checks 'did:' prefix and 3+ parts but missing: (1) 2048 char limit, (2) regex validation, (3) PLC fast-path optimization, (4) method name validation (lowercase only). Does not validate the identifier portion strictly.
atproto.impact: Interop: May accept invalid DIDs that strict services reject.
atproto.reproducibility: Test with malformed DIDs exceeding limits

atproto.details: Local Handle parser has basic validation but missing: (1) 253 char limit, (2) strict regex, (3) segment length validation (63 chars max), (4) TLD validation for disallowed TLDs. Does normalize to lowercase which is correct.
atproto.impact: Interop: May accept handle formats that ATProto services reject.

atproto.details: No TID (Timestamp ID) parsing or generation implemented. Indigo has full implementation including ParseTID with 13-char validation, TIDClock for monotonic generation. TIDs are used for record revision tracking.
atproto.impact: Functionality: Cannot validate or generate TIDs for record versions. Limited ability to enforce ordering on commits.
atproto.reproducibility: Grep for TID in pkg/atproto/ - not found

atproto.details: Local collectionAndRKey function correctly splits path but depends on non-compliant NSID/RecordKey parsers.
atproto.impact: Functionality: Path parsing depends on broken NSID/RecordKey parsers.

atproto.details: MST implementation uses simplified construction (buildMST creates flat entries, not properly fanout-based tree). No tests verify fanout calculation.
atproto.impact: Interop: May generate MSTs that don't match Indigo's fanout structure. Could cause verification issues.
atproto.reproducibility: Inspect buildMST function and compare to Indigo

atproto.details: Missing implementations for: sync.getBlocks, sync.getCheckout, sync.getHead, sync.getLatestCommit, sync.getRecord, sync.getHostStatus, sync.getRepoStatus, sync.listBlobs, sync.listHosts, sync.listRepos, sync.listReposByCollection, sync.notifyOfUpdate, sync.requestCrawl.
atproto.impact: Functionality: Limited sync capabilities. Cannot do partial repo fetches or status checks.
atproto.reproducibility: Grep pkg/atproto/ for these method names

atproto.details: RepoGetRecord does NOT validate that 'repo' is valid at-identifier format, 'collection' is valid NSID format, or 'rkey' is valid record-key format before making request. Trusts server to validate.
atproto.impact: Interop: May send malformed requests that get rejected. Could fail earlier with better validation.

atproto.details: Firehose correctly decodes CBOR frames with header+payload structure. Properly handles ErrorFrame and all current message types.
atproto.impact: Positive: Firehose decoding is sound for implemented message types.

atproto.details: LexBlob correctly handles both modern and legacy formats.
atproto.impact: Positive: Can interop with both old and new blob formats.

ssb.details: ToBencode() correctly returns []interface{}{header, signature}. Fixed in compliance review 2026-04-06.
ssb.impact: Core feed format for metafeeds now functional.

ssb.details: Sign() correctly encodes only ContentSection[0] and prepends 'bendybutt' prefix.
ssb.impact: Signatures now interoperable with other BendyButt implementations.

ssb.details: BFE encodes feed addresses (0x00), messages (0x01), signatures (0x04), generic strings (0x06). Fixed in April 2026.
Correctly parses {origin, portal, target}, verifies origin is caller, portal is room, target has announced. Forwards duplex stream.
ssb.impact: Peers can establish tunnelled connections through the room.

ssb.details: streamAttendants correctly sends initial state with {type:'state', ids:[...]} then streams joined/left events.
ssb.impact: Clients can discover online peers for tunnel connections.

ssb.details: Returns {name, membership, features} but 'membership' is boolean (caller is member) rather than string privacy mode. INTENTIONAL design decision.
ssb.impact: Clients expecting 'open'|'community'|'restricted' string will be confused, but boolean is pragmatic for bridge use case.

ssb.details: Implemented but has role reversal issue. RequestSolution is called BY server ON client (per spec), but local impl appears to be client-side handler. Missing sendSolution implementation.
ssb.impact: HTTP authentication for SSB peers needs verification with actual SSB clients.
ssb.reproducibility: Review auth.go and compare to SIP-009

ssb.details: Note.MarshalJSON encodes -1 for non-replicate, or (seq<<1)|(!receive). StateMatrix tracks frontiers per peer.
Correctly parses args array, supports id, seq, limit, live, keys flags. Emits {key, value, timestamp} objects.
ssb.impact: Legacy replication compatible with classic SSB clients.

ssb.details: Full HTTP invite flow implemented: serveJoinPage generates claimURI, handleInviteConsume accepts JSON body, validates token, checks denial, consumes invite.
